meta.source: OA crosswalk Slice B scenarios A–D

scenarios.title: Scenario A: British Telecom plc — Dual 144A/Reg S Notes with Put Option
scenarios.offering: £400 million notes issued by a UK-incorporated entity, eligible for resale under both Rule 144A to QIBs and Regulation S to non-U.S. persons. Book-Entry-Only with a 3-year put option. Lead underwriter is a DTC Participant. Transfer Agent is Euroclear Bank.

walkthrough.label: UK Entity
walkthrough.text: Check "Yes." OA § I.B.1.b requires a UK/Ireland LOR rider for each U.K./Ireland issuer security (list all CUSIPs on the rider).

walkthrough.label: Article 8 Security
walkthrough.text: Yes — notes are securities under NY UCC Article 8.

walkthrough.label: Registration
walkthrough.text: Check BOTH Rule 144A and Regulation S. Dual eligibility is common for global issuers.

walkthrough.label: BEO
walkthrough.text: Yes — Book-Entry-Only. Requires fully executed BEO LOR (BLOR or ILOR) on DTC’s form before eligibility (OA § I.B.1.a).

walkthrough.label: Put/Tender
walkthrough.text: Yes — 3-year put. Tender LOR on the form is for certificated put/tender issues. This deal is BEO — do not assume Tender LOR is automatic.

walkthrough.label: LOR package
walkthrough.text: (1) Standard Issuer LOR/BLOR, (2) UK/Ireland rider (required), (3) non-U.S. currency rider (required for GBP). Distinct restricted CUSIP(s) for 144A/Reg S (OA § II.A.1.b).

walkthrough.label: Signatures / timing
walkthrough.text: Non-U.S. issuer — original manual signatures commonly required (§ I.A.6); Exhibit B: draft LOR ≥ 10 BD, original hard copy by 3 BD pre-close.

walkthrough.label: CUSIP
walkthrough.text: Restricted 144A/Reg S CUSIP(s) must be distinct from unrestricted notes of the same class.

walkthrough.label: Currency
walkthrough.text: GBP — non-U.S. currency rider is required in addition to the UK/Ireland rider.

walkthrough.label: Agent Letter + Confirmation
walkthrough.text: Agent Letter on file before eligibility; Agent Confirmation by email within 10 business days after closing (OA § I.A.1).

walkthrough.label: OFAC
walkthrough.text: Screen issuer/principals; sanctioned issuers are not DTC eligible (OA § I.D).

scenarios.takeaway: Maximum complexity under OA § I.B: UK/Ireland rider and non-U.S. currency rider are both required for a GBP UK issuer; BEO needs BLOR/ILOR with Exhibit B timing; 144A/Reg S needs distinct restricted CUSIP(s). Tender LOR is a certificated-form concept — don’t bolt it onto BEO without checking.

scenarios.title: Scenario B: Acme Corp. 8.500% Senior Notes due 2030 (Rule 144A)
scenarios.offering: $500 million high-yield notes sold only to QIBs under Rule 144A. Distinct CUSIP from any registered notes. Lead underwriter is a DTC Participant.

walkthrough.label: Issue Type
walkthrough.text: Check "Corporate."

walkthrough.label: Registration
walkthrough.text: Check "Eligible for resale under Rule 144A of the '33 Act."

walkthrough.label: CUSIP table
walkthrough.text: One row (term bond, type "T"). Distinct CUSIP/CINS from unrestricted securities of the same class (OA § II.A.1.b).

walkthrough.label: LOR / riders
walkthrough.text: BLOR or ILOR for BEO. OA expressly requires LOR riders for older 144A/Reg S issues; use current DTCC forms for new issues.

walkthrough.label: PORTAL
walkthrough.text: PORTAL is retired; private QIB venues replaced it. Distinct CUSIP remains mandatory.

walkthrough.label: Electronic Submission
walkthrough.text: UW SOURCE/UWC package per Exhibit B (≥ 6 BD pre-close).

walkthrough.label: Certificate Sizing
walkthrough.text: $500 million exactly — one certificate is acceptable; multi-certificate rule applies when a maturity exceeds $500M (OA § I.B.1.d).

scenarios.takeaway: For Rule 144A, never reuse an unrestricted CUSIP. Distinct restricted identification is non-negotiable under the OA.

scenarios.title: Scenario C: Thames Industries plc — SEC-Registered Notes
scenarios.offering: UK-incorporated issuer doing a registered offering of $300 million notes under Form F-3. Lead underwriter is a DTC Participant. Transfer Agent is Computershare.

walkthrough.label: UK Entity
walkthrough.text: Check "Yes." UK/Ireland LOR rider required (list CUSIPs on the rider).

walkthrough.label: Registration
walkthrough.text: Check "Registered with the SEC."

walkthrough.label: Foreign issuer
walkthrough.text: OA § I.B.3 — DTC may require special representations and/or a foreign legal opinion.

walkthrough.label: LOR
walkthrough.text: BEO LOR (BLOR/ILOR) plus UK/Ireland rider. USD registered offering: no currency rider; no 144A restricted-CUSIP split unless also sold under those exemptions.

walkthrough.label: Signatures / timing
walkthrough.text: Non-U.S. — original manual signatures commonly required; Exhibit B non-U.S. LOR draft ≥ 10 BD, original hard copy by 3 BD pre-close.

walkthrough.label: Agent Letter + Confirmation
walkthrough.text: Agent Letter on file before eligibility; Agent Confirmation within 10 business days after closing.

walkthrough.label: OFAC
walkthrough.text: Screen issuer/principals; OA § I.D bars eligibility for OFAC-sanctioned issuers.

scenarios.takeaway: Foreign issuers add layers. The UK rider, potential foreign legal opinion, and original signature requirement can each add days — start the LOR process early.

scenarios.title: Scenario D: State of Franklin General Obligation Bonds, Series 2025A
scenarios.offering: $250 million GO bond with serial maturities from 2026–2035, issued as Book-Entry-Only. Lead underwriter is a DTC Participant. Transfer Agent is U.S. Bank.

walkthrough.label: Issue Type
walkthrough.text: Check "Municipal." Do NOT check "Limited Underwriting" because principal exceeds $1 million.

walkthrough.label: Article 8 Security
walkthrough.text: Yes — municipal bonds are securities under NY UCC Article 8.

walkthrough.label: UK Entity
walkthrough.text: No — issuer is a U.S. state.

walkthrough.label: Registration
walkthrough.text: Municipal securities are exempt from SEC registration under Rule 3(a)(2). Check "Exempt under Rule 3(a)(2)."

walkthrough.label: CUSIP Table
walkthrough.text: 10 rows (one per serial maturity). Each gets its own CUSIP, interest rate, and maturity date. Type code = "S" for Serial.

walkthrough.label: BEO
walkthrough.text: Yes — BEO LOR required (OA § I.B.1.a). Check for an existing BLOR under the exact issuer legal name.

walkthrough.label: Serial CUSIPs
walkthrough.text: OA § II.A.1 — each municipal serial/term needs a distinct CUSIP printed on the certificate(s).

walkthrough.label: NIIDS (muni data path)
walkthrough.text: In UW SOURCE, input NIIDS Data Elements and select Disseminate Security Information. Separate from the eligibility request (OA § I.C.3; Exhibit B Note 2).

walkthrough.label: Put/Tender
walkthrough.text: No — standard GO without optional put/tender.

walkthrough.label: Agent Confirmation
walkthrough.text: Transfer Agent emails confirmation within 10 business days after closing (OA § I.A.1); Agent Letter must already be on file.

scenarios.takeaway: Serial maturities multiply CUSIP workload; each maturity is its own DTC security. Confirm BLOR name match, run the eligibility package on Exhibit B timing, and complete NIIDS dissemination as parallel tracks.
